edges.description: Mutant H3 K27M sequesters EZH2/PRC2 complex
edges.target: PRC2 Complex Inhibition and H3K27me3 Loss

edges.description: Loss of H3K27me3 derepresses developmental and oncogenic genes
edges.source: PRC2 Complex Inhibition and H3K27me3 Loss
edges.target: Epigenetic Derepression and Aberrant Gene Activation

edges.description: Epigenetic changes block normal differentiation programs
edges.source: Epigenetic Derepression and Aberrant Gene Activation

nodes.description: Global loss of H3K27me3 causes derepression of genes normally silenced during development, including proliferative and stemness genes. Paradoxically, some loci show retained or increased H3K27me3, suggesting complex redistribution rather than simple loss.
nodes.id: Epigenetic Derepression and Aberrant Gene Activation

nodes.description: Lysine-to-methionine substitution at position 27 of histone H3 (K27M) occurs in H3F3A (encoding H3.3) or HIST1H3B/HIST1H3C (encoding H3.1). Despite being present in only 5-15% of total H3 molecules, the mutant histone acts as a dominant negative, sequestering and inhibiting the PRC2 complex.

evidence.reference_title: Early distant progression in adult Histone-3 K27-altered diffuse midline gliomas.
evidence.snippet: H3 K27-altered diffuse midline glioma (DMG) is a molecularly defined, highly aggressive tumor entity since the 2016 revision of the World Health Organization (WHO) classification of tumors of the central nervous system (CNS).

nodes.description: Epigenetic dysregulation prevents normal glial differentiation, maintaining cells in a proliferative, undifferentiated state. This contributes to the aggressive behavior and treatment resistance of these tumors.

The H3 K27M mutant histone binds to and inhibits EZH2, the catalytic subunit of PRC2, which normally trimethylates H3K27. This results in global loss of H3K27me3, a repressive epigenetic mark, leading to aberrant gene activation and loss of cell identity.
nodes.id: PRC2 Complex Inhibition and H3K27me3 Loss

biological_processes: negative regulation of gene expression, epigenetic
